unverified.label: Slower AI Development Pace
unverified.quote: Look, it may be a good thing that it’s not as fast. There’s a whole bunch of other things that we need to think through with this technology... We don’t have a lot of time to sort out before we get to [Artificial General Intelligence].

unverified.label: The AlphaZero Generalization Process
unverified.quote: AlphaZero was the next version... it doesn’t need any human data either because what it does, it starts off completely random. So you imagine a blank slate, neural network... it plays itself millions and millions of times, different versions of itself.

unverified.label: In Silico Drug Discovery Pipeline
unverified.quote: we can sort of do a virtual screen... keep modifying the compound so that it has less and less side effects... only at the final stage do you check it in the wet lab.

unverified.label: Tabula Rasa (Blank Slate) Learning
unverified.quote: removing any knowledge human crafted knowledge both in the data and in the uh any of the kind of huristics that we've given the system... starts off random uh to begin with. It it just it only has the rules of the game.

unverified.label: CERN-like Collaboration
unverified.quote: using the scientific method very carefully very precisely very thoughtfully... on making sure each step we understood each step... as we got to the final goal of building AGI

unverified.label: The One-Way Commons
unverified.quote: if there's a full sharing of everything, then um uh progress overall is faster, right? ... but look, it may be a good thing that that it's not as fast as that because there's safety to worry about.

unverified.label: Use Games as Proving Grounds
unverified.quote: I thought was the perfect training ground for AI systems to make quick progress... it's more convenient to have a metric that you can hill climb against.

unverified.label: Plan for Success Decades in Advance
unverified.quote: We planned for success. In 2010, no one was thinking about AI let alone AGI. But we already knew that if we could make progress... the technology created would be unbelievably transformative.

unverified.label: Look to Neuroscience for Principles, Not Prototypes
unverified.quote: It’s a mistake also to ignore the brain... There are hugely important insights and general principles that you can use in your algorithms.

unverified.label: Scaling is an Artform
unverified.quote: Scaling laws also don't just work by magic... You have to adjust the recipe and that's a bit of an art form.

unverified.label: Search In Silico, Validate In Vitro
unverified.quote: we can sort of do a virtual screen... keep modifying the compound so that it has less and less side effects... only at the final stage do you check it in the wet lab.

unverified.label: AGI Requires Exceptional Care
unverified.quote: I think a technology as transformative as AGI will be... akin to something like the invention of fire or electricity requires exceptional care and foresight

unverified.label: The Highest Level of Creativity
unverified.quote: I think that's the highest level of scientific creativity... actually coming up with the question in the first place or coming up with the theory or the hypothesis.

all_results.label: AI as the Ultimate Meta-Solution
all_results.quote: It could also help with science and physics and help experts in those areas crack the problems and the questions they care about. So it seemed to be like the perfect meta solution in a way.

all_results.label: AI as the Ultimate Scientific Tool
all_results.quote: The reason I've spent my whole career on AI is is the upsides of solving basically the ultimate tool for science and understanding the universal around us.

all_results.label: Precautionary Principle for AGI
all_results.quote: I think a technology as transformative as AGI will be um you know I think this will be akin to something like the invention of fire or electricity requires exceptional care and foresight

all_results.label: Intelligence Requires Generalization
all_results.quote: For me, learning is synonymous with intelligence and always has been.

all_results.label: The Brain as the Ultimate Benchmark
all_results.quote: The brain is the only existence proof we have that we know of in maybe in the universe uh that general intelligence is possible. So that for me is the bar for what AGI should be.

all_results.label: AI as an Engineering Science
all_results.quote: I call it an engineering science, because unlike the natural sciences, you have to build the artifact of interest, first, and then, once you have it, you can then use the scientific method to reduce it down and understand its components.

all_results.label: Classical Computing's Universal Modeling Capability
all_results.quote: Any pattern or structure that can be generated or found in nature can be efficiently discovered and modeled by a classical learning algorithm.

all_results.label: Compute for Algorithmic Experimentation
all_results.quote: A new innovation, a new invention, is only useful if it can also scale.

all_results.label: Games as the Ideal Proving Ground
all_results.quote: The other advantage of games, of course, is that they’ve been built to be challenging to humans, and you can use top human players as a great benchmark. The other thing is they have clear objectives—you have to win the game or to maximize the score.

all_results.label: Hypothesis Generation as Peak Creativity
all_results.quote: actually coming up with the question in the first place or coming up with the theory or the hypothesis. I think that's much much harder and I think that's the highest level of scientific creativity

all_results.label: Sensory-Motor Grounding for True Intelligence
all_results.quote: For an AI to be truly general, to build AGI, we feel that the AGI system needs to understand the world around us and the physical world around us, not just the abstract world of languages or mathematics.

all_results.label: The Necessity of a Slower, Coordinated Pace
all_results.quote: if we can maybe it would be good to have a bit of slow a slightly slower pace than we're currently predicting even my timelines so that we can get this right society

all_results.label: Transformers Are Insufficient for AGI
all_results.quote: the transformer architecture... will underpin, be one of the main components of a future AGI system. But my prediction is it's not going to be enough on its own.

all_results.label: AI Benefits Outweigh Energy Costs
all_results.quote: they're going to be orders of magnitude more uh uh improvement and saving than they use in terms of the energy costs

all_results.label: AI for Scientific Breakthroughs
all_results.quote: One of the only ways to tackle climate in today’s fragmented political world is to come up with some new technologies. I actually think AI as an industry needs to be doing more of that.

all_results.label: Broad Societal Debate on AI
all_results.quote: AI is going to affect everything, so I think it does need to be debated, by all parts of society...

all_results.label: Games as the Ideal Microcosm
all_results.quote: a microcosm of something important in real life... And we get to practice it many times in an environment that’s serious, but not serious, in a sense.

all_results.label: Information as the Fundamental Unit
all_results.quote: I think information is really the right way to understand the universe.

all_results.label: Neuroscience as an AGI Roadmap
all_results.quote: It’s a mistake also to ignore the brain, which a lot of machine learning people do. There are hugely important insights and general principles that you can use in your algorithms.

all_results.label: Scientific Method over Silicon Valley Ethos
all_results.quote: I think we should be using the scientific method to do that, be thoughtful and hypothesis-generate and try and get a better understanding of our things rather than just maybe the sort of Silicon Valley trope of 'Move fast and break things.'

all_results.label: Shared Machinery of Memory and Imagination
all_results.quote: I therefore hypothesised that the same neural machinery responsible for reconstruction might also support ‘constructive’ cognitive functions such as imagination.

all_results.label: Slower AI Development Pace
all_results.quote: Look, it may be a good thing that it’s not as fast. There’s a whole bunch of other things that we need to think through with this technology... We don’t have a lot of time to sort out before we get to [Artificial General Intelligence].

all_results.label: Deep Reinforcement Learning (Model-Guided Search)
all_results.quote: Finding the optimal solution in an enormous combinatorial space. Learn a model of that environment (from data or simulation). Use that model to guide a search according to an objective function

all_results.label: Criteria for a Suitable AI Problem
all_results.quote: What makes for a suitable problem for AI? Massive combinatorial search space. Clear objective function (metric) to optimise against. Either lots of data and/or an accurate and efficient simulator

all_results.label: The AlphaZero Generalization Process
all_results.quote: AlphaZero was the next version... it doesn’t need any human data either because what it does, it starts off completely random. So you imagine a blank slate, neural network... it plays itself millions and millions of times, different versions of itself.

all_results.label: AI Problem Suitability Framework
all_results.quote: It's massive combinatorial space. You need to try and build a model that understands the physics and the chemistry of the natural phenomena. And then... use it to do a very efficient search

all_results.label: DeepMind's AI Development Pipeline
all_results.quote: You start with the research question and find that answer. Then we do some major neuroscience and then we look at it in machine learning and we implement a practical system... and then that’s ready to scale.

all_results.label: Hybrid / Neurosymbolic Systems
all_results.quote: some of our most important work combines neural networks and deep learning with things like Monte Carlo research.

all_results.label: In Silico Drug Discovery Pipeline
all_results.quote: we can sort of do a virtual screen... keep modifying the compound so that it has less and less side effects... only at the final stage do you check it in the wet lab.

all_results.label: Planning atop LLMs
all_results.quote: On top of that, we're working on things like AlphaZero-like planning mechanisms on top that make use of that model in order to make concrete plans to achieve certain goals in the world.

all_results.label: Scene Construction
all_results.quote: ...the process of ‘scene construction’, defined as the generation and maintenance of a complex and coherent spatial context.

all_results.label: Tabula Rasa (Blank Slate) Learning
all_results.quote: removing any knowledge human crafted knowledge both in the data and in the uh any of the kind of huristics that we've given the system... starts off random uh to begin with. It it just it only has the rules of the game.

all_results.label: Targeted Data Gathering via Frontier Modeling
all_results.quote: you see where does that system overall perform well where does it not perform well and then usually where it doesn't perform well that's because you need more data

all_results.label: Capability Overhang
all_results.quote: I think a kid these days could probably start a multi-billion dollar business in some ways using these tools in some new way that no one had thought about.

all_results.label: Digital Biology
all_results.quote: Biology at its most fundamental level can be thought of as an information processing system albeit a phenomenally complex and emergent one

all_results.label: Root Node Problems
all_results.quote: If you think of the tree of all knowledge. These are kind of root node problems which if you cracked it, it would unlock a whole branch of new research or new applications.

all_results.label: The Brain as an AGI Prototype
all_results.quote: We have a prototype of this — the human brain. We can tie our shoelaces, we can ride cycles and we can do physics with the same architecture. So we know this is possible.

all_results.label: 10x Industrial Revolution
all_results.quote: I sometimes quantify like AGI at the coming of AGI as like 10 times the industrial revolution at 10 times the speed. So unfolding over a decade instead of a century.

all_results.label: Balancing Serving with Training
all_results.quote: We’re now also in the era of full commercialization of these systems, so you’ve also got to balance serving with training.

all_results.label: AI for Climate Change
all_results.quote: One of the only ways to tackle climate in today’s fragmented political world is to come up with some new technologies. I actually think AI as an industry needs to be doing more of that.

all_results.label: Balancing Serving and Training
all_results.quote: We’re now also in the era of full commercialization of these systems, so you’ve also got to balance serving with training.

all_results.label: Decline of Open Research
all_results.quote: There’s so much commercial pressure, so some of those things can’t be shared quite as openly anymore, which is a shame on the one hand, but it’s understandable.

all_results.label: Early Days of AI
all_results.quote: There were only a few dozen researchers working on AI properly and we all knew each other. We were, in most cases, really good friends.

all_results.label: Hardware Bottlenecks
all_results.quote: Right now, it’s memory chips, but it’ll probably be something else tomorrow.

all_results.label: Learning is Intelligence
all_results.quote: For me, learning is synonymous with intelligence and always has been.

all_results.label: Scientific Method over Silicon Valley Trope
all_results.quote: I think we should be using the scientific method to do that, be thoughtful and hypothesis-generate and try and get a better understanding of our things rather than just maybe the sort of Silicon Valley trope of 'Move fast and break things.'

all_results.label: The Highest Level of Creativity
all_results.quote: I think that's the highest level of scientific creativity... actually coming up with the question in the first place or coming up with the theory or the hypothesis.

all_results.label: The Pace of AI
all_results.quote: One year in AI feels like 10 years. So, I don't know if we want it to go any faster.

all_results.label: Understanding Reality
all_results.quote: I want to use AI as a tool to help us understand the nature of reality around it.

all_results.label: Unparalleled Potential of AI
all_results.quote: I’ve dedicated my career to advancing AI because of its unparalleled potential to improve the lives of billions of people.

all_results.label: Assuming Transformers Are Enough
all_results.quote: the transformer architecture... will underpin, be one of the main components of a future AGI system. But my prediction is it's not going to be enough on its own.

all_results.label: Baking Human Heuristics into AI
all_results.quote: Something's obviously not quite right about the definition of intelligence... in the sense of like no human, you could imagine a human grandmaster um not being able to learn how to play tic-tac-toe.

all_results.label: Racing Without Coordination
all_results.quote: if we can maybe it would be good to have a bit of slow a slightly slower pace than we're currently predicting even my timelines so that we can get this right society

all_results.label: A/B Testing AI in the Wild
all_results.quote: I think it's important we use more the scientific method than, perhaps, lean on normal sort of technology, A/B testing out in the wild.

all_results.label: Diluting AGI for Marketing
all_results.quote: I don't think AGI should be sort of turned into a marketing term or for commercial gain.

all_results.label: Focusing on Frivolous Entertainment
all_results.quote: I actually think AI we need AI to help us with some of the uh the pressing challenges facing society including in health and in in in energy

all_results.label: Ignoring the Brain
all_results.quote: It’s a mistake also to ignore the brain, which a lot of machine learning people do.

all_results.label: Ignoring the Cost of Research Secrecy
all_results.quote: There’s so much commercial pressure, so some of those things can’t be shared quite as openly anymore, which is a shame on the one hand, but it’s understandable.
